source_title: CA FAIR Plan Rate Increase 2026: What Homeowners Must Know
subject: A European angle on your CA FAIR Plan Rate Increase 2026: What Homeowners Must Know piece
body:
Hi Mariah,

Your breakdown of the CA FAIR Plan rate increases was genuinely useful — the way you walked through what the changes mean for everyday homeowners, not just the policy mechanics, made a complicated subject feel approachable and real.

My Villa builds Italian-designed homes in Los Angeles using reinforced exposed concrete (cemento a vista), a construction method long favored across the Mediterranean for its resilience and longevity. Given how much insurability now hinges on structural integrity and fire resistance, that building tradition feels increasingly relevant to conversations like the one you started.

Would you be open to receiving more background on the project, or a brief call with our founder Paolo Mezzalama? No pressure either way — happy to share materials first if that's easier.

Best,
Lisa Monelli
My Villa Media Team
info@myvilla.la · myvilla.la

My Villa builds Italian-designed homes in Los Angeles using reinforced exposed concrete (cemento a vista), a material tradition rooted in Mediterranean climate-adaptive architecture. body:
Hi David,

Your piece on fire-resistant ADU construction in San Jose caught my attention — the way you traced how builders are rethinking materials from the ground up, not just retrofitting, felt like a genuinely different angle on a conversation that usually stays surface-level.

My Villa builds Italian-designed homes in Los Angeles using reinforced exposed concrete — cemento a vista — a Mediterranean tradition where structural resilience and aesthetic intention have always been the same thing. That philosophy maps closely onto what California builders are now being pushed toward by necessity.

Paolo Mezzalama, our founder, would love to share how this approach translates practically in an LA context. Would you be open to a short call or receiving some background materials?

Best,
Lisa Monelli

Hi Leonor,

Your piece on the Stone Villa was a genuinely enjoyable read — the way you captured that tension between Tuscan materiality and the raw scale of the California mountains felt spot-on, and the photography you selected made the case beautifully.

My Villa is a Los Angeles-based company building Italian-designed homes in reinforced exposed concrete — cemento a vista — and the Mediterranean sensibility you described in that project sits very close to what Paolo Mezzalama, our founder, has been exploring here in LA. There is a real conversation to be had about why that architectural language translates so naturally to the California landscape.

Would you be open to receiving some background materials, or perhaps a short call with Paolo when your schedule allows?

Your piece on the Safeco non-renewal wave was genuinely useful — the step-by-step guidance on avoiding a coverage gap is exactly what California homeowners need right now, and it's clear you understand the insurance landscape here better than most.

My Villa builds Italian-designed homes in Los Angeles using reinforced exposed concrete — a method rooted in Mediterranean construction that happens to meet California's most demanding structural and fire-resistance standards. source_title: Insulated Concrete Forms (ICF): 12 Essential Pros & Cons
subject: A European angle on your Insulated Concrete Forms (ICF): 12 Essential Pros & Cons piece
body:
Hi Bethany,

Your breakdown of ICF construction on newhousebuilder.com was genuinely thorough — I appreciated how honestly you weighed the upfront cost concerns against the long-term performance gains, rather than giving readers the usual one-sided enthusiasm.

My Villa builds Italian-designed homes in Los Angeles using reinforced exposed concrete (cemento a vista), bringing a Mediterranean building tradition where mass concrete construction has been trusted for generations. That cultural confidence in concrete as a permanent, living material feels directly relevant to the durability and resilience conversation your article opens up.

I wanted to reach out and ask whether you would be open to receiving some background materials, or a short conversation with our founder Paolo Mezzalama, if concrete construction in the LA market interests you.

Best,
Lisa Monelli
